headline: Sector HIGH_142 projected to exceed capacity in 18 minutes.
summary: Convective weather is blocking the eastern corridor and pushing 22 flights into a single high-altitude sector. Utilization peaks at 118% of capacity. A westbound meter or reroute relieves the breach but shifts load toward HIGH_144.

agents.title: Sector HIGH_142
agents.detail: Peak overload projected in 18 minutes. 22 contributing flights.

agents.title: Echo Top
agents.detail: Convective weather blocking eastern corridor. Propagation exceeds historical pattern by 38%.

agents.title: Network Impact
agents.detail: Arrival banks at ORD and EWR will inherit delay if unmitigated.

agents.title: Similar Scenarios
agents.detail: Western reroute observed success rate 83% across matched precedents.

agents.title: Confidence Check
agents.detail: Storm propagation exceeds historical match by 38%. Historical confidence reduced 91% to 87%.

recommendations.title: Meter or Reroute Westbound Flow
recommendations.summary: Meter westbound flow before the HIGH_142 peak and route via the western corridor.
recommendations.expected_impact: Relieves HIGH_142 to ~96% capacity; ~21% delay reduction.
risks: Shifts 12% capacity burden to adjacent sector HIGH_144.

support: Air Marshal and Weather Boy agree on cause
8 historical precedents favor western reroute (83% success)
weaknesses: Delay estimate is an inspectable heuristic, not a certified prediction

divergence_alarm.reason: Auditor notes storm propagation exceeds historical match by 38%. Historical recommendation confidence reduced from 91% to 87%.
